title: hippocrepis scabra var. commutata
intro: Hippocrepis scabra var. commutata, also known as the hairy broom or hairy needle-broom, is a plant species that belongs to the legume family and is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its alkaloid and saponin content. The health benefits of Hippocrepis scabra var. commutata include its potential to treat various conditions such as digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, as well as its antiseptic and antifungal properties. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its leaves and flowers can be used fresh or dried for medicinal purposes.
